title: Recovery Services Vault without immutability + Backup Contributor compromise
narrative: A Recovery Services Vault has no immutability policy AND soft-delete is shorter than the backup retention policy. A ransomware operator who escalates to Backup Contributor simply deletes recovery points before encrypting primary data — no restore path remains.

steps.action: Delete recovery points; soft-delete expires quickly.
Encrypt primary data; victim has no backup.

title: Backup policy retention shorter than compliance requirement
narrative: A backup policy retains daily backups for 30 days but the regulatory requirement is 7 years. During an audit, the gap is revealed — or during a legal hold, historical data required for discovery cannot be produced.

steps.action: Backups rotate out at 30 days.
Requested data unavailable.

title: Azure Backup cross-region replication disabled
narrative: Backup vault is configured for LRS (local-redundant) only — no cross-region replication. A regional Azure outage or regional disaster means no recovery path; the entire backup estate is offline along with primary infrastructure.

steps.action: Primary + backup region become unavailable.
Extended RTO beyond stated DR objective.

title: Site Recovery RPO higher than business tolerance
narrative: Azure Site Recovery RPO is 4 hours for a system whose tolerance is 15 minutes. A failover loses 4 hours of transactions; incident post-mortem reveals the mismatch and triggers a costly re-architecture.

steps.action: Failover to Site Recovery replica.
steps.gain: Service restored but with data loss.

steps.action: 4 hours of transactions lost; customer SLA breached.

title: Recovery Vault without Multi-User Authorization
narrative: Recovery Services Vault does not require a second approver for destructive operations (MUA disabled). A single compromised Backup Contributor can delete recovery points unilaterally — no approval workflow, no four-eyes control.

steps.action: Delete recovery points in a single action.
Proceed with ransomware knowing no backup recovery is possible.

title: Automation Account runbook stores secrets in clear variables
narrative: An Azure Automation Account runbook uses plaintext variables (not encrypted variables) to store service account passwords. Anyone with Automation Operator role can read the variables.

steps.action: GET variables via Automation API; fields are plaintext.
Authenticate with stolen creds.
steps.gain: Service account access across the environment.

data_at_risk: Wherever the service account is authorised

title: Update Management exclusions for prod hosts
narrative: Prod hosts are excluded from Update Management schedules to 'avoid outage risk'. They accumulate critical CVEs for months. One in-the-wild exploit yields fleet-wide compromise because the patches were deliberately skipped.

steps.action: High-impact CVE with public exploit.
Attack unpatched fleet; every excluded host is vulnerable.

title: Instant Restore capacity exhausted — delayed recovery
narrative: Azure Backup Instant Restore capacity is below the amount needed to restore prod workloads within the target RTO. During a real incident, restores queue behind capacity limits; SLA blown.

steps.action: Initiate recovery; capacity unavailable.
RTO exceeded waiting for capacity.

title: Backup agent version stale on prod VMs
narrative: Azure Backup agent (MARS / MABS) on prod VMs is running a version from 18 months ago. A published vulnerability in the agent itself lets an attacker escalate privilege on the host during backup operations.

steps.action: Exploit backup agent vulnerability.
steps.gain: Privilege escalation to SYSTEM on the VM.

steps.action: Read any file on the host; access MI tokens.

services_at_risk: Any cloud resource the VM's MI can reach

title: DR plan untested — silent replication failures
narrative: No DR test has run in 12+ months. Replication has silently broken for some workloads (schema changes, new disks unprotected). During a real failover, restoration fails for the very workloads that matter most.

steps.action: Configuration drift accumulates unchecked.
Failover reveals: critical DBs have no recent replica.
